definition: The structural inflation floor has shifted upward due to housing shortage (3.5-5.5M units), deglobalization, and healthcare cost inflation. Direction is supported by multiple independent lines, but the honest confidence interval of approximately [-10bp, +100bp] includes zero when methodology uncertainty and correlated errors are incorporated.
evidence: commodities_analyst_02 adds 5-10bp from agricultural structural factors
quant_researcher_01 demonstrates honest CI of [-10bp, +100bp] incorporating methodology uncertainty
Additive decomposition (housing 10-20bp + deglobalization 10-20bp + healthcare 10-15bp) implies false precision due to correlated component errors
open_questions: Are the component errors (housing, deglobalization, healthcare) correlated, and by how much?
Does the lower bound genuinely include zero?
What observable data would narrow the confidence interval?
